description: "Nano Banana Pro" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview model family (https://blog.google/technology/ai/nano-banana-pro/)
fp_reason: Nano Banana Pro is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

description: "Nano Banana" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-2.5-flash-image model family (https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/gemini/how-nano-banana-got-its-name/)
fp_reason: Nano Banana is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-2.5-flash-image image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

description: Uses deprecated Live model gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md marks this family deprecated and directs migration to gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview

send_client_content used to send a mid-session user message; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 62 states this method is only supported for seeding initial context history and must not be used for new user messages
suggested_fix: Use session.send_realtime_input(text=...) for mid-session user messages; reserve send_client_content for seeding history only

description: send_realtime_input(media=Blob(...)) uses the media key; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md lines 65-66 explicitly prohibit this and require specific keys (audio/video/text)
Variable model_id passed to client.models.generate_content(model=model_id, ...) is never defined in the snippet; reproducing this code raises NameError at runtime
suggested_fix: Replace model_id with a literal model string consistent with the rest of the file (e.g., "gemini-3-flash-preview")

description: Section "When MCP is NOT Installed (Fallback Only)" instructs the agent to "Use fetch_url to:" — backticked as a tool name; sibling skill gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 266 uses agent-neutral phrasing "Use web fetch tools to:" which works across Claude Code (WebFetch), Cursor, Codex CLI, and other host agents
suggested_fix: Replace "Use `fetch_url` to:" with "Use web fetch tools to:" to match the sibling skill's agent-neutral phrasing

description: Link text reads "thought circulation" but URL anchor is #signatures and the documented term is "thought signatures" (SDK exposes signature field, referenced in gemini-interactions-api/SKILL.md line 246)

Vertex skill names the documentation MCP tool search_documents; gemini-api-dev, gemini-live-api-dev, and gemini-interactions-api all name it search_documentation; an agent loading both receives different tool names for what reads like the same operation
suggested_fix: Standardize on one tool name across all four skills, or document the deliberate distinction explicitly

description: Vertex skill lists gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio as the primary Live model; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 29 marks the 2.5 family deprecated and designates gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview as the sole recommended Live model
suggested_fix: Align Vertex's live-API guidance with the live-api-dev skill, or document Vertex's deliberate divergence with rationale

description: Instructional MCP example uses StdioServerParameters(command='npx', args=['-y', '@philschmid/weather-mcp']); -y silently accepts the latest published version of a third-party npm package not maintained by Google; reproduced verbatim, a supply-chain compromise of that package would execute in user environments
suggested_fix: Replace with a Google-owned or known-good MCP server example, or add a warning comment that third-party MCP servers should be audited and version-pinned before production use

description: Example uses media=Blob(...) key explicitly prohibited by gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 65; doc consistency defect with no security impact
suggested_fix: Update example to use audio=types.Blob(...) per the sibling skill
